title: Jessie Buckley Wins Golden Globe for Hamnet
text: Irish actress Jessie Buckley claimed the best actress in a drama award at the Golden Globe Awards for her role as Agnes Shakespeare in director Chloé Zhao's film adaptation of Maggie O'Farrell's novel Hamnet. Buckley's victory was described as expected, solidifying her position as a frontrunner for the upcoming Academy Awards. Her co-star Paul Mescal, who plays William Shakespeare, did not win in the best supporting actor category, losing to veteran Stellan Skarsgård for Sentimental Value. The film itself achieved a notable upset, winning best drama film over the heavily favored Sinners directed by Ryan Coogler. Upon receiving her award, Buckley expressed her emotions candidly: This is not a normal feeling or situation to be in, but thank you, Golden Globes. She highlighted the international scope of the production, noting it featured a Chinese director, a lot of Irish and a mostly Polish crew telling the story of one of history's most famous figures. Industry observers anticipate both Buckley and Australian actress Rose Byrne—who won best actress in a comedy or musical—will compete against each other in the lead actress race at the Academy Awards, with nominations announced January 22nd.
